{{Quote|Falud died fighting for ''freedom...'' trying to overthrow a despicable ''tyranny''!|Leia Organa|<ref name="world" />}}
'''Falud''' was a [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]]<ref name="geonosis" /> [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent who operated on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Although Falud was a committed Rebel willing to die for his cause, his son [[Choyd]] did not share his sympathies, and instead fell in with a notorious delinquent gang known as the [[Freelies]]. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Falud was captured by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] along with Rebel leaders [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and another Rebel agent named [[Paxin]]. However, the group was freed from their bonds by Skywalker's [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and managed to overcome their [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guards and seize their weapons. The foursome was soon rushed by another group of stormtroopers during their escape attempt, and Falud was struck by a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt and [[Death/Legends|killed]].<ref name="world" />

==Behind the scenes==
Falud made his first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in ''[[Gambler's World]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written and illustrated by [[Russ Manning]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="world" />
